title: Revisions of Reno Municipal Code Section 8.32 (Trees and Shrubs)
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: March 27, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Nathan Ullyot, Director
Matt Brezina, Parks Manager
DEPT: Parks and Recreation Department
SUBJECT: Revisions of Reno Municipal Code Section 8.32(Trees and Shrubs)
Background
In early 2025, Parks and Recreation Department staff began a review and update of Title 8 Section 32 of the Reno Municipal Code (RMC 8.32) regarding the promotion and protection of trees in public spaces. The goal of this effort is to modernize language and provide the Urban Forester with the tools and protection needed to preserve Reno's urban canopy.
The language drafted by Parks and Recreation Department staff was presented to the Reno Urban Forestry Commission in December for feedback and recommended changes. Comments were positive and constructive, and those changes were included in the final draft submitted to the City Attorney's Office (CAO) for review in January. CAO comments were provided to Parks and Recreation Department staff, and changes have been implemented.

Next Steps
Parks and Recreation Department staff is moving through the steps that are appropriate to bring this update to City Council. Those steps include:
• Urban Forestry staff presented the recommended changes to Department heads on March 16, highlighting some of the more substantial changes that may affect other departments' operations and stakeholder groups.
• Department heads are expected to submit constructive feedback and recommendations by March 27, including any suggestions for further public outreach that aligns with recent code updates.
• Parks and Urban Forestry staff will present the update at Council policy briefings in April.
• Parks and Urban Forestry staff will subsequently coordinate public outreach initiatives with stakeholder groups.
• At the conclusion of public outreach Parks and Urban Forestry staff will incorporate appropriate changes and begin the process of Urban Forestry Commission recommendation and Council Adoption.
• Council adoption will follow which will require a first and second reading of the ordinance before adoption is complete.

fields.authors: Nathan Ullyot, Director; Matt Brezina, Parks Manager
fields.tldr: The Parks and Recreation Department is updating Reno Municipal Code Section 8.32 (Trees and Shrubs) to modernize language and strengthen protections for the urban canopy. The draft was reviewed by the Reno Urban Forestry Commission in December and the City Attorney's Office in January, with feedback incorporated. Department heads are being consulted before the update proceeds to Council policy briefings in April. Public outreach, Urban Forestry Commission recommendation, and two Council readings are required before final adoption.
fields.key_decisions: Council will ultimately need to adopt the revised ordinance following a first and second reading; no immediate vote is requested at this stage.
fields.action_items: Department heads to submit feedback and recommendations by March 27, 2026; Urban Forestry staff to present update at Council policy briefings in April; staff to coordinate public outreach with stakeholder groups; incorporate public outreach feedback; obtain Urban Forestry Commission recommendation; proceed to Council adoption via two readings.

projects: Revisions of Reno Municipal Code Section 8.32 (Trees and Shrubs)

December [2025] – Draft presented to Reno Urban Forestry Commission for feedback
January [2026] – Final draft submitted to City Attorney's Office for review
March 16, 2026 – Urban Forestry staff presented recommended changes to Department heads
March 27, 2026 – Deadline for Department heads to submit feedback
April [2026] – Presentation at Council policy briefings
people: Nathan Ullyot, Director (Parks and Recreation Department)
